summary: The pull request modifies the authentication logic by changing the error handling in the login function. Previously, the function would return nil when authentication failed, but now it returns an error. Additionally, the JWT token generation and validation process has been updated to use a new method signature that takes a context parameter.

findings.title: Breaking Change in Error Handling
findings.comment: The login function previously returned nil on authentication failure but now returns an error. This is a breaking change that will affect all callers expecting a nil return value, potentially causing runtime panics or incorrect error handling in dependent code.

findings.title: JWT Token Generation Signature Change
findings.comment: The GenerateToken function signature has changed to accept a context parameter. This change may require updates to all call sites and could introduce compatibility issues if not all callers are updated to pass the context parameter correctly.

findings.title: Missing Context Propagation in Validation
findings.comment: The ValidateToken function signature was updated to accept a context, but the actual implementation does not appear to use context cancellation or timeout features, which may reduce the robustness of token validation in high-concurrency scenarios.

suggestions: Update all call sites of the login function to handle the new error return value properly
Verify that all callers of GenerateToken and ValidateToken functions are updated to correctly pass context parameters
Add comprehensive tests to ensure that error conditions in authentication are properly handled by callers
